title: Rich Korean Pork Ssam
description: A resinous and shattering Korean dish featuring golden caster sugar and pickle, perfect for a brunch mood. This ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly recipe is served in a ssam form on a lazy-susan-spinner vessel.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together the golden caster sugar, Korean chili flakes, soy sauce, and rice vinegar.
Place the pork belly in a large bowl and pour the sugar and chili flake mixture over it, making sure to coat it evenly.
Let the pork belly marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Remove the pork belly from the marinade and place it on a baking sheet lined with aluminum foil.
Roast the pork belly in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
While the pork belly is roasting, mix together the minced garlic and grated ginger in a small bowl.
After the pork belly has roasted for 20 minutes, brush the garlic and ginger mixture over it.
Continue to roast the pork belly for an additional 10 minutes, or until it is crispy and golden brown.
Remove the pork belly from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes.
Slice the pork belly into thin strips and serve it in a ssam form with the chopped pickled radish and lettuce leaves on a lazy-susan-spinner vessel.
